title: Activity limitation documentation

examination.text: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
examination.rationale: Supports entered treatment burden, goals, values and priorities for MSK activity-limitation documentation; it does not generate a decision.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities

medication.text: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.
medication.rationale: Supports entered daily-activity, mobility and falls context for MSK activity-limitation documentation; it does not generate a decision.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Affected activity, baseline, onset, body region, pain, strength, range, mobility aids, work demands and goals are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for MSK activity-limitation documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
